page: Microsoft Azure F16as_v6 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the Fasv6 family. It features 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. The processor includes 32 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache per core, alongside support for advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, AVX512_VNNI, AVX512_BF16, and SHA_ni. The instance is configured with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB per core.

This instance runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It does not include local storage, though it is Premium Storage capable, requiring external block storage attachment for persistent data. There are no onboard GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses included with this configuration.

In benchmark evaluations, the F16as_v6 demonstrates strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Geekbench text processing scoring in the top tier. Single-threaded and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, Geekbench image processing, and PassMark memory scores fall into the top 25% tier. Multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations perform within the average range. For machine learning tasks, the server achieves top-tier text generation speeds on small language models, though performance scales down to average levels for medium and large models.

Given its dedicated AMD EPYC cores and high clock speed, the F16as_v6 offers a balanced resource density for workloads requiring consistent compute performance without the overhead of hyperthreading. It is well-suited for applications such as text processing, image processing, lightweight language model inference, data decompression, and general-purpose web serving.
description: Microsoft Azure F16as_v6 is a general-purpose server instance from the Fasv6 family, powered by a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9V74 processor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs on the x86_64 architecture, paired with 64.0 GB of system memory. Running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance does not include local storage or GPUs but supports Premium Storage. Benchmarks reveal top-tier single-core CPU performance and text processing capabilities, alongside strong memory performance and image processing speeds. Multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations align with average industry standards. This configuration is highly suitable for workloads such as text and image processing, lightweight language model inference, data decompression, and general-purpose application hosting.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F16as_v6 is a general-purpose server featuring 16 dedicated AMD EPYC 9V74 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and top-tier single-core CPU performance, ideal for text processing and lightweight inference workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F16as_v6 is a general-purpose server with 16 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and strong single-core performance.
tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC server featuring high clock speeds and balanced memory for efficient text processing and general-purpose compute workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated AMD EPYC 9V74 physical cores running at 3.7 GHz
64.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per core
Top-tier single-core CPU and text processing benchmark performance
Premium Storage capable with no local storage or onboard GPUs
Optimized for text processing, image processing, and lightweight LLM inference
